arguments.description: file to read the passphrases from

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# nasty -h
 -a x	set minimum length of passphrase
 -b x	set maximum length
 -m x	set guessing mode:
 	incremental: try them all
 	random: try at random
 	file: read phrases from file (use -i)
 -i x	file to read the passphrases from
 -f x	file to write the found passphrase to
 -c x... charset, one or more from the following:
 	a: a-z
 	A: A-Z
 	0: 0-9
 	.: all ascii values (32...126)
 	+: 32...255 (default(!))
 -k x	filter string to select a key
 -v 	enable verbose mode

local_help.man:
NASTY(1)                         User Commands                         NASTY(1)

NAME
     nasty - A tool which helps you to recover your GPG passphrase

SYNOPSIS
     nasty [OPTIONS]

DESCRIPTION
     nasty is a program that helps you to recover the passphrase of your PGP or
     GPG-key in case you forget or lost it.

OPTIONS
     -a x   set minimum length of passphrase

     -b x   set maximum length

     -m x   set guessing mode:
               incremental: try them all
               random: try at random
               file: read phrases from file (use -i)

     -i x   file to read the passphrases from

     -f x   file to write the found passphrase to

     -c x...
            charset, one or more from the following:
               a: a-z
               A: A-Z
               0: 0-9
               .: all ascii values (32...126)
               +: 32...255 (default(!))

     -k x   filter string to select a key

     -v     enable verbose mode

     -h     show command options

ISSUES
     Nasty will not work if you try it with a gpg-agent running in your system.
     For  obvious  reasons the agent will ask you the passphrase to access your
     private key - which you probably don't record, right? :)

AUTHOR
     This manual page was written by Tiago  Bortoletto  Vaz  <tiago@debian.org>
     for the Debian GNU/Linux system (but may be used by others).

nasty                            September 2009                        NASTY(1)
local_help.apropos: nasty (1)            - A tool which helps you to recover your GPG passphrase

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=32219196365d54cc0dd4a42cde42b04d64242d59, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
tool which helps you to recover your GPG passphrase
Nasty is a program that helps you to recover the passphrase of your PGP or
GPG-key in case you forget or lost it. The following features will make things
easier:
- set minimum/maximum length of the passphrase
- incremental mode, random mode or reads a file for guessing
- charset filter
.
This package is useful in forensics investigations.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
